reviews.review_title: One of the best sports simulators
reviews.review_description: One thing I love about this game is the competitive level you can find. Although they're still polishing some moves and physics, the developers are keeping a close eye on things to fix any issues. In short, the game is highly recommended for players who want to learn or practice tennis.

developer_response.body: Hi Thessa, so nice reading your kind lines. Thanks a lot!

reviews.review_description:
The game is super fun. I play it whenever I can. But there are some annoying parts to the game.

Most of the features require premium or elite. Because I’m a free player, I have match cooldowns, require access keys for some gameplay modes, no tours, no rematches, no ai commentator, and more. These are basic features in many esport games, yet in this game they’re not incorporated.

Also, there are so many times where the opponent would hit the ball and my character wouldn’t automatically teleport to where the ball would land, so I’d have no way of reaching the ball and the opponent would get the point. Sometimes, that’s the match point too. It’s so so frustrating because it’s hard to play and win matches.

If a moderator sees this, please fix your game!

developer_response.body:
Thanks for this constructive and extensive feedback. Good points! well taken.
I would love to give you more insights and also say thankyou with a little surprise.
Please email me gregory@vr-motion-learning.com

reviews.review_description:
Update:  Thank you for fixing the flicker-spectator glitch! As I said before, this is an amazing app. I play it 3-4 times a week and have gotten exponentially better. It's a terrific workout. And the sunshine has made everything much easier to see. Great job!!! 
Previous review:
After the last Meta Quest 3 update some of the players are just black silhouettes.  I like the new stadium and sunlight, but the spectators flicker between black or red silhouettes causing a nausearing strobe effect. This was one of my favorite games. I own the premium version. I sent video to Meta because I couldn't find a way to send them to you.

 Please fix this.

developer_response.body: Hi, thanks so much for upgrading your review. We are happy to have you on board. Hope you enjoy many more fun and competetive matches on the virtual courts!

reviews.review_description: Rain, cloud, or sun, Tennis esports is a game I keep coming back to. It's truely a lot of fun and helps keep me out of the chair and active - particularly during those winter months. It's got a casual side along with a more serious competitive tournament side, mix that in with as much social interaction you want, and it's just like a trip down to the clubhouse on a beautiful summers day for a game of tennis. Lots of tutorials and settings to bring new players up to speed, and seasoned players constantly improving. Only downside is that time flys by too quickly and I have to pop the headset onto charge before I know it.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for these wonderful and motivating lines. So nice to read.
I wish you lots of fun and great matches on the virtual court :-)

reviews.review_description:
From the very first match, I was blown away. This is, without exaggeration, the most realistic tennis experience I have ever had in a video game. extremely appreciated.

The immersion is next level. During intense rallies, I completely forget I’m playing a game. Highly, highly recommended. 🎾🔥

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for these kind lines. The team appreciates it!
Lots of fun on the virtual courts :-)

reviews.review_description: I never go a day without playing this addictive court game, my left controller is actually broken, for the fact that I can still play using my right controller while using hand tracking movement for secondary control is absolutely game changer. Even though, you can't walk around using your hand but I'll love to see hand movement in next update. Surely recommended game 🎯💯🔥

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for this kind review!

reviews.review_title: One of the best games ever
reviews.review_description: This game is incredibly addictive and competitive, if you love sports games you'll be hooked instantly, Tennis Esports also has a very friendly and welcoming community that is always trying to help out, once you start you always come back.

developer_response.body: So nice to read your lines. Thanks so much!  We are honored having you on board. Wish you many fun matches on the virtual court :-)

reviews.review_description:
I use the the toggle to move to the net when I want to go there but then the game moves me to a different position and I’m not able to hit the ball at the net which I wanted to do. 

Plus you have people returning a ball at 80 MPH by just flicking their wrist at the point of contact but if you do and actual swinging motion with speed you get around 60 plus mph. 

The real like swing needs to generate more power than just flicking your wrist.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much Max and congrats to your unbelievable performance on the court!

reviews.review_description: I play tennis for school and I really enjoy coming home and being able to play tennis in virtual reality! Ive tried other tennis games and this one is by far the best!! Such great experiences here.

reviews.review_description: This delivers an immersive and exhilarating tennis experience that brings the court right to your living room. With realistic physics, responsive motion tracking, and stunningly detailed environments, this game offers a near authentic feel of playing professional tennis.

reviews.review_title: Greatest Game on Earth Just Ask Stan,
reviews.review_description:
Greatest Game on Earth™
(Just Ask Stan, Edn4, and Masterjohneu)

If you’re looking for the most welcoming, transparent, competitive, ego free, totally not alt-account-infested esports environment on planet Earth — congratulations. You’ve found it.
Just ask Stan.
Ask Edn4.
Ask Masterjohneu.
They’ll tell you how friendly it is.
This is the only competitive space where:
The same handful of names rotate the top spots like it’s a family-owned timeshare.
“Pro” apparently means “professional at flicking exploits.”
Alternate accounts multiply faster than rabbits in mating season.
Private DMs are apparently a contact sport.
But don’t worry — it’s all very organic.
Totally normal that:
Nobody cracks the ceiling unless someone “takes a break.”
Public call-outs are brushed off as “competitive spirit.”
Personal bashing becomes “community engagement.”
Silence from moderation equals “balanced governance.”
Just part of the culture.
And let’s talk about the “Pro” label.
Nothing screams elite competition like:
Drama dominating gameplay.
Whisper campaigns in DMs.
Strategic gaslighting dressed up as banter.
And a leaderboard that feels less like skill and more like a gated HOA community.
But hey   great vibes.
You want realism?
Other games give you full body movement, legs engaged, actual athletic immersion, sweat pouring down your spine.
Here?
You get wrist flick Olympics and Discord gladiator school.
Incredible innovation.
And the best part?
If you question it   you’re the problem.
If you call it out. you’re “toxic.”
If you leave. they call you sick and  “unstable.” And want to make you better like a doctor diagnoses
If you stay   you’re content.
That’s not competition.
That’s a containment strategy.
But sure.
“Great game.”
Just ask Stan.
Ask Edn4.
Ask Masterjohneu.
They’ll tell you everyday what a Divkhead you are.

developer_response.body:
Hi my friend David.  Thanks for resetting this. We cannot change others people behavior.
All we can do is to do our best ourselves.

reviews.review_title: Good for playing for fun, Badforpractice
reviews.review_description:
Got to play this for an hour. First off the tutorial has your racket set to your right hand, with no way of changing it until the tutorial is over.
 Second off, you get a limited amount of these keys to use for basically everything, AI practice, arcade matches, practice mode, basically everything. And once those keys run out. There is basically nothing to do, only thing to really do is unranked matches, but  after a handful of matches the game puts you on a cooldown timer, I don’t want to wait to play some tennis.
Third off, the match experience is awful, getting hand tracking mode was hard enough, but the manual serving just sucks, always so laggy and buggy, and the thumb stick movement just sucks, if you try to go into a opposite direction you start slowing down, stop for at least half a second then start going the direction you pointed to with said thumb stick. Also, where is the accessibility to use the backhand with 2 hands? I didn’t know tennis was a one hand sport.

It’s a fun game if you just got a meta/oculus quest headset and are looking for a game to play for fun. 

But for an intermediate tennis player, this is not the game to do practice with. 3/10.
I didn’t expect to sweat this much! After 20 minutes, it actually feels like a real match. The immersion is next levelyou forget you’re in VR. Great for both fun and fitness.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for your kind lines!

reviews.review_title: Great tennis came which could be amazing
reviews.review_description: I only gave this 4 stars because of two things which are currently letting this down, but I think could be easily improved to make this 5 stars.  Firstly the serving, about 40% of the time the racket goes through the ball which can be really frustrating.  The other thing is when playing a ranked person there is no option to do a search, so every game is played on the hard court - there are search option icons but they are currently not working, so hopefully they can activate this feature soon.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the kind review and all your comments. May we help you with all your issues? Would like to investigate , if the serve problem arises from weak a weak internet connection or something else. Same with the search option.
Please write us to.   support@vr-motion-learning.com

reviews.review_title: Good physics but no depth for SP
reviews.review_description:
The alternative Tennis games( First person Tennis) offer a career mode with tours and rankings. That’s what this game needs, It has the decent visuals and physics, But no depth to keep you playing. Would be the best Tennis game on the Quest if it gave you a reason to play it. 

Arcade mini games are fun for five minutes. You want to play it more, But you lose interest with no depth. 

Hope they can add something to it though, As they’ve nailed a lot of other aspects.

Update; If me and my friend both own the game, Why does one of us need a key to play against each other online? It’s not even a product we own in that case, Social hub isnt the answer. Putting it behind a paywall is scandalous, Hence why no one is ever on the servers.
